rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes when concrete bounds are provided
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or auxiliary variable
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scopes; use them only in component type annexes
Ensure AGREE assumptions and guarantees refer to valid component ports or declared variables
Use exact Boolean expressions only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope; otherwise omit the clause
generic_rules: Use named constants or visible relations for bounds in range assumptions and guarantees
Avoid direct use of subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Prefer component type annexes for assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports
Use implementation annexes only for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Ensure helper declaration types align with the expression types to avoid type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `eq` for equation declarations and `assign` for implementation-level bindings
Boolean expressions should be used only when the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Avoid direct use of AADL property names as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantees in implementation scopes
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Avoid direct use of AADL property names as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed
